description: Nuclear Restoration Services (NRS) is responsible for safely decommissioning 14 nuclear sites across the UK. NRS works with the Nuclear Decommissioning Authority .

details.body:
Nuclear Restoration Services (NRS) is responsible for safely decommissioning 14 nuclear sites across the UK.

NRS works with the Nuclear Decommissioning Authority.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Almost £2 million was invested by NRS last year in socio-economic initiatives, supporting good causes in communities local to its 14 decommissioning sites.
ordered_featured_documents.title: NRS socio-economic funding supports 129 UK community projects

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Hunterston B nuclear power station in North Ayrshire has transferred ownership from EDF to the Nuclear Decommissioning Authority.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Hunterston B nuclear power station has transferred to the NDA

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Office for Nuclear Regulation grant a site licence to Nuclear Restoration Services (NRS) - major milestone that will enable the site’s decommissioning.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Two weeks to go: Hunterston B achieves licence milestone

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Almost 200 containers of low-level radioactive waste that once formed part of Britain’s experiment with fast breeder nuclear reactors are being entombed.
ordered_featured_documents.title: First containers of waste from Dounreay reach permanent disposal

ordered_featured_documents.summary: NDA group puts decommissioning at the forefront of the agenda at Nuclear Week in Parliament
ordered_featured_documents.title: Decommissioning underpins ambitions for homegrown clean energy

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Powered systems that controlled airflow and ventilation through the two reactors at Wylfa nuclear power station, Anglesey have been permanently switched off.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Wylfa reactors breathing naturally
